question.label: Mentoring useful
question.name: Mentoring feels useful — what is it giving you right now that the core role isn't?
question.description: Drills into why mentoring has become the place he feels useful, to surface what feels missing elsewhere.

answer: I am not sure the work I am doing is the work that gets recognized.

assessment.note: He doubts that his current work is the kind that gets recognized, which signals mild negative growth trajectory; also reveals a clarity/recognition concern worth noting.

new_queue.label: Recognized work
new_queue.name: What kind of work do you think gets recognized here?

new_queue.label: Support needed
new_queue.name: What would help this feel more workable over the next couple of weeks?

history.answer_excerpt: I have been less present in rituals, yes.
I have been less present in rituals, yes.
After the promotion decision, I was not clear on what really needed to change.
I stayed focused on delivery because that felt controllable.
Mentoring has been one place where I still feel useful.
Mentoring has been one place where I still feel useful.
I am not sure the work I am doing is the work that gets recognized.
